title: oroya peruviana var. baumannii
intro: Oroya peruviana var. baumannii, also known as Baumann's cinchona, is a variety of the Oroya peruviana plant, which belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of the parasite that causes Chagas disease, a serious and potentially life-threatening condition. The health benefits of Oroya peruviana var. baumannii include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
